questions.clue:
[IMAGE CLUE]
(Kelly of the Clue Crew stands by a fountain.) I'm on the University of Wisconsin campus in this city, which opened in 1849 with 17 students & now has 40,000

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jimmy of the Clue Crew walks through a parklike area with numerous white headstones.) I'm at this 600-acre site that was home to Robert E. Lee until the start of the Civil War

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jimmy of the Clue Crew walks down a long corridor.) I'm in one of the longest buildings in the world at SLAC, the Linear Accelerator Center of this California university

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Kelly of the Clue Crew stands on the lawn of a neoclassical building.) I'm at this famous home which in 1987 was named to the World Heritage Sites list along with the nearby University of Virginia

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew stand on a naval dock.) I'm here on San Diego Bay at the Naval Amphibious Base that bears the name of this Spanish explorer, whose men discovered the Grand Canyon
Helpful phrases: "llame a la policia" (call the police) or "llame a los bomberos" (call these emergency workers)
Shortly after "¿Donde esta el baño?", you might have to ask, "¿Tienes mas papel higienico?" do you have more of this?
"Tengo mucha hambre. Por favor deme espagueti y albondigas" means "I'm hungry" & requests this dish
En una iglesia, you might hear, "Bendiceme, padre, porque he pecado", this confessional phrase
questions.answer: Bless me, Father, for I have sinned

questions.clue: Shopping vocabulary es muy importante: "¿Cuanto cuesta esta cadena de oro?" How much does this cost?
It "makes the man" & "makes the pot boil"; love of it is also "the root of all evil"
A shiro-maku is a simple, white one of these robes worn by a Japanese bride at her wedding
The 5th generation of this iPod included a built-in video camera, FM radio & a pedometer
A specialty of New Orleans, a muffuletta is one of these
In Judaism, it's a scroll with verses from Deuteronomy inserted in a small case & attached to a doorpost

"Stormin' Norman" of the 1990s
The high-flying "Red Baron" of World War I
Mr. "Unconditional Surrender" of the Civil War

Hominy grits
Moussaka

From "Seinfeld": "Marcy comes over & she tells me... her ex-boyfriend was over... last night &" this this this "I'm really tired today"
This *NSYNC song hit the top 5 in 2000; see ya!
It precedes "voom" in an Art Carney TV catchphrase
In 1983 Paul McCartney & Michael Jackson spent 6 weeks at No. 1 with this hit
Give the full name of this "Catch-22" officer; if you add his rank you'll be saying it 4 times fast

final.clue: She's tied with Mariah Carey for most No. 1 hits by a female with 18: 12 as a member of a group & 6 as a solo act
